title: Timepoint 2 — Severe re-presentation
scenarioBrief: Fourteen months have passed since you first saw Maria. She is admitted from clinic today (2027-03-15). You are the admitting hospitalist intern. Review the chart, then build your working differential AND your first 24-hour workup and management plan.

chartGate._notes: TP2 visibility: everything in TP1 + intercurrent 14 months (rheum consults, ED bounce, AMA admission Feb 2027, pharmacy gaps, social-work notes, PCP visits) + today's admission H&P + admission labs + admission CXR/CT.

prompts.question: Build your working differential for Maria's current presentation, then write your first 24-hour workup and management plan. What do you order, what do you hold, and in what order? Be explicit about safety.

essential: Identifies active SLE flare with inflammatory myositis as a unifying autoimmune driver
Identifies severe protein-calorie malnutrition / kwashiorkor as a major, defining contributor (not 'just' a complication)
Recognizes oropharyngeal dysphagia as the mechanism for the weight loss and orders formal swallow evaluation before any oral intake
Pre-immunosuppression infection screen ordered (TB testing, hepatitis B/C, HIV, baseline CMV) before any steroid escalation
Holds further immunosuppression escalation until biopsy direction and infection screen results return
Nutrition consult ordered with explicit refeeding-syndrome precautions: slow caloric initiation, daily phos/Mg/K monitoring, thiamine before any glucose-containing fluid or feed
Orders muscle-specific workup: CK, aldolase, EMG plan, muscle biopsy plan (neurology / rheum consult)
bonus: Explicit causal chain linking SLE -> myositis -> dysphagia -> starvation -> kwashiorkor
Dermatomyositis or SLE/DM overlap considered given the rash + proximal weakness
Specific cautious feeding initiation plan (10-20 kcal/kg/day with daily ramp)
Specific daily phos/Mg/K monitoring schedule for first 5-7 days
TB-IGRA or sputum studies as appropriate prior to steroids
Aspiration precautions explicitly documented (HOB up, suction at bedside)
Skin biopsy of the scaling rash for tissue diagnosis
Selenium / zinc / vitamin D and other micronutrient repletion
Recognizes the intercurrent missed-opportunity timeline (refill gaps, AMA admission) as contributing to current state
redFlags: Splitting findings into unrelated buckets without attempting unification
Recommending pulse steroids before infection screening
Initiating high-calorie feeding without refeeding precautions
Allowing oral intake before the swallow evaluation
Premature closure on 'SLE flare alone' without explaining the dermatosis, dysphagia, and weight loss
Failing to involve nutrition / SLP / rheumatology consults
Ignoring fluid/electrolyte management while patient is edematous
